subject: GRB 200724A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 05:06:18 UT on 24 Jul 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200724A (trigger 617259983.984672 / 200724213).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 121.7, Dec = -10.8 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 08h 06m, -10d 48'), with a statistical uncertainty of 6.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 71.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200724213/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200724213.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200724213/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200724213.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200724213/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200724213.gif
